Case Study 01 · Design Leadership · 2024–Present

Driving Enterprise
Margin Through
Design

How I self-initiated a platform project to drive adoption of value-added services, changing how an engineering driven organization thinks about design.

Role
Lead Product Designer
Team
3 → 5 Designers + Research & Content
Company
Braintree / PayPal
Timeline
2024 – Present
$1.5T
Annual transaction volume processed by Braintree, much of it lower-margin than PayPal's branded offerings.
50%+
Monthly merchant login rate, disproving the org's core assumption that merchants don't use the control panel.
3→5
Designers on the team, plus a dedicated UX researcher and content designer secured through demonstrated impact.
Payment Tools — Explore and enable value-added services
Fraud Protection Advanced — Product detail and enablement
Integration Setup — Guided onboarding and progress tracking
Fraud Protection Filters — Rules configuration and protection summary
Performance Dashboard — Fastlane conversion rate analytics
Settings — Enabled payment tools and merchant configuration
1 / 6
01
Context

The Opportunity
the Org Missed

In 2024, Braintree's company OKRs were set to increase transaction margin by driving adoption of value-added services. The org had a mandate. But no one thought to include design.

Braintree processes over $1.5 trillion in annual transactions, making it one of the biggest payment processors on the planet. But much of that volume runs at lower margin than PayPal's branded offerings. The strategy was clear: get merchants to adopt value-added services (VAS). What wasn't clear was how.

The org had a mandate with no execution strategy. I identified the gap and moved.

I identified the gap between company goals and execution. Then closed it.

02
Discovery

What We
Found

I took my team and scoped a project to map the entire end-to-end process for enabling a value-added service. What we discovered was alarming.

  • There was no self-service experience. Enabling a VAS required manual intervention at every step.
  • Merchants had no visibility into what services existed, let alone why they'd want them.
  • There was no pricing transparency. Merchants couldn't evaluate ROI before committing.
  • Merchants had no tools to build a business case and present a VAS to their own leadership.
  • Adoption was driven entirely by random chance encounters with the right sales rep at the right time.

This wasn't a sales problem or a product gap. It was a design problem, and design was exactly positioned to fix it.

BT VAS Baseline Journey Map — End-to-end merchant lifecycle mapping

03
Platform Design

The End-to-End
VAS Framework

I architected a comprehensive platform: a new surface within the merchant control panel designed to support the entire VAS adoption lifecycle.

1
Discover
Learn

A surface where merchants could discover which value-added services exist and understand why they matter to their business.

2
Decide
Evaluate

Transparent pricing and clear value propositions, giving merchants the tools to build an internal business case without needing a sales call.

3
Activate
Enable

Digital agreements and self-service enablement, so a merchant could turn on a service without picking up the phone.

4
Launch
Configure

Guided technical configuration to get from "enabled" to "live" without needing a solutions engineer on speed dial.

5
Prove
Measure

Real-time performance dashboards showing ROI, making the value of a service visible and defensible to merchant leadership.

6
Grow
Expand

Contextual cross-sell opportunities for adjacent services, turning the platform into a growth engine, not just an onboarding tool.

This wasn't a set of features. It was a strategic platform designed to make VAS adoption continuous, measurable, and scalable across every Braintree merchant.

04
Influence

Shifting the Org
Through Results

Without a formal mandate, I built the case through demonstrated outcomes. The org followed.

I started with a handful of willing partners. Once those features shipped and showed results, others took notice. PMs who had never engaged with design before, who viewed product work as purely an engineering exercise, began requesting our help.

The demand for design grew faster than our capacity. We went from invisible to sought-after. The team grew from 3 to 5 designers and expanded to include a dedicated UX researcher and content designer, all as a direct result of demonstrated impact.

Design didn't get invited to the table. We built our own table. Then everyone else pulled up a chair.

05
Impact

Results &
What Changed

The org had accepted certain things as fact. We disproved them with data.

Assumption Busted: "Merchants don't log in"

  • More than 50% of merchants logged in every month, far more than anyone assumed.
  • After launching the new VAS framework, repeat login rates increased, giving merchants a reason to come back.
  • The control panel became a product in its own right, not just a configuration utility.

Business Outcomes

  • Drove adoption of multiple margin-boosting products across the merchant base.
  • Merchants reported positive ROI from enabled services, validated through our performance dashboards.
  • Proved that self-service adoption is possible at enterprise scale in a segment previously considered too complex for it.

Org Impact

  • Design went from an afterthought to a sought-after partner within the LE org.
  • Multiple PMs who had never engaged with design began proactively requesting collaboration.
  • Reusable frameworks were adopted by other product teams for their own launches.
06
Takeaways

What This
Demonstrates

SkillStrategic Vision
Identified a company-level OKR gap that design was positioned to address, before anyone asked.
SkillSystems Thinking
Designed a full lifecycle platform rather than isolated features, scaling across multiple products and teams.
SkillCross-Functional Influence
Shifted the org's perception of design from optional to essential through results, not advocacy alone.
SkillTeam Building
Grew the design team from 3 to 5 and secured dedicated research and content design resources by proving ROI on design investment at enterprise scale.
SkillDesign Leadership
Led a team through a self-initiated project with no mandate, delivering results that changed the org's roadmap.
Design didn't wait for a seat
at the table
We built one
Garrett Galayda · Braintree / PayPal · VAS Platform · 2024–Present
